rounds.blurb: The airship Hindenburg burns while landing at Naval Air Station Lakehurst, New Jersey.
rounds.story: The largest aircraft ever built burned in about half a minute while landing; remarkably, 62 of the 97 aboard survived. Filmed, photographed, and narrated live on radio, the disaster ended the airship era overnight.

rounds.blurb: Times Square, deserted at midday in the first spring of the pandemic.
rounds.story: New York was then the pandemic's global epicenter, losing hundreds of people a day while the Crossroads of the World stood empty at noon. The billboards kept playing to nobody.

rounds.blurb: Elvis Presley meets Richard Nixon in the Oval Office — the National Archives' most-requested photograph.
rounds.credit: Ollie Atkins / White House — public domain
rounds.story: Presley arrived unannounced at the White House gate with a handwritten letter asking to be made a federal agent-at-large in the war on drugs. Nixon gave him the badge — and the resulting photo became the National Archives' most requested item, ahead of the Constitution.

rounds.blurb: The Women's March on Washington, the day after the presidential inauguration.
rounds.story: With sister marches on every continent — including a boat off Antarctica — the day drew an estimated four million participants in the U.S. alone, likely the largest single-day protest in American history.

rounds.blurb: Perseverance's selfie with the Ingenuity helicopter at Jezero Crater, Mars.
rounds.story: Perseverance posed with Ingenuity, a four-pound helicopter that days later made the first powered flight on another planet — carrying a swatch of the Wright Flyer's wing fabric. Rated for five flights, it flew 72.
